news.title: TSMC’s Results Weren’t a Great Sign for Apple
news.summary: Apple’s top chip-making partner needs it less and less these days.  TSMC, the Taiwanese chip manufacturing giant that makes the main processors for the iPhone and Apple’s other products, posted strong first-quarter results on Thursday that included its highest gross margin in more than 20 years.  This could help explain why Apple’s share price slipped around 1% on Thursday following TSMC’s results, even as most other tech stocks rallied.

news.title: Jamie Dimon Sells $40 Million of JPMorgan Stock
news.summary: JPMorgan Chief Executive Jamie Dimon sold more than $40 million of the bank’s stock on Wednesday in the latest cash out of his shares.  Dimon has run the bank for over two decades and didn’t start selling any of his bank stocks until the end of 2023 when he sold a million shares worth around $180 million.  Dimon, his wife and his family trusts hold the shares.

affected_positions.rationale: Direct positive analyst commentary on Amazon's growth trajectory and business expansion supports the 8% portfolio position.
Positive sentiment toward Magnificent Seven stocks creates modest tailwind for fellow mega-cap tech holding.

source_event.title: Sarat Sethi is watching this 'Magnificent Seven' stock

signals.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon sold $40 million in company stock, his second major sale since late 2023. This follows a previous $180 million sale and may signal caution about valuation or personal portfolio diversification.

affected_positions.rationale: Insider selling by long-tenured CEO typically signals concern about near-term valuation or market conditions, though may also reflect personal financial planning.

source_event.title: Jamie Dimon Sells $40 Million of JPMorgan Stock
